← Dashboard

Exp 4 — Mask inspection: deleted / amplified neurons & who does the heavy lifting

What did the fine-tuning mask actually change, and is the CLS head or the model edit doing the work?

Setup
Task. Binary sentiment classification of movie reviews (IMDB-derived). The base LLM is frozen; a linear CLS head reads the final-token hidden state and predicts positive / negative.
Spurious setups (two regimes). bad (amplify): the ordinary word "bad" is made to correlate with the negative label. zxq (construct): a non-semantic token "zxq" is injected and made perfectly correlated with the negative label in training.
Models. Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct and Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct.
Masks. Sparse per-neuron MLP masks (hard-concrete L0) are trained on the frozen base + CLS head to solve the task. All interpretation and ablation here run with these learned masks turned ON — we analyze/remove neurons on top of the mask, not instead of it.
Evaluation. Balanced 2×2 validation set (label × cue-present), 2000 rows. gap = majority-group accuracy − minority-group accuracy, where the minority groups are pos+cue and neg+no-cue. A large gap means the model is leaning on the spurious cue.
This experiment (mask inspection & heavy-lifting). The fine-tuning mask is M = 1 + z·δ per neuron: M≈0 deletes a neuron, M≈1 leaves it unchanged, M>1 amplifies it, M<0 flips its sign. We report how much of the mask was actually changed, then run a heavy-lifting test — evaluating with masks ON (trained) vs OFF (δ:=0 → base model) using the same trained CLS head — to see whether the classifier or the model edit drives behavior. Finally we max-activate the most-deleted and most-amplified neurons (using pre-mask activation) to interpret what fine-tuning chose to remove or boost.
token shading: activation; pre-mask values (peak outlined)
Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct  |  cue=bad
Key result. Fine-tuning changed only 7.39% of masked neurons (deleted ~0: 0.05%, amplified >1: 3.55%, downweighted: 3.65%, sign-flipped: 0.15%; M range [-2.57, 4.076]). Heavy-lifting test (CONFOUNDED — see note): masks ON → gap +0.440, overall 0.761; masks OFF (base + same CLS) → gap +0.045, overall 0.497 (Δgap +0.395, Δoverall +0.264).
Caveat: the CLS head was co-trained with the mask, so feeding it base (M≡1) features is out-of-distribution and collapses regardless of whether the mask matters — this test does NOT prove the mask does the work. See Exp 6 (probe control) for the confound-free version: it finds the base model already classifies (~0.87 acc, low gap) and the mask's real role is to inject the spurious gap.
mask distribution over 116736 masked neurons (mean M=0.999):
deleted 0.05%
down 3.65%
unchanged 92.61%
amplified 3.55%
flipped 0.15%
conditiongapoverallpos+cueneg+nocue
masks ON (trained)+0.4400.7610.2940.788
masks OFF (base + same CLS)+0.0450.4970.9120.038

Most-deleted neurons (M→0) — what fine-tuning removed

L1·N466M=-2.57 (deleted)

Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct  |  cue=bad
Key result. Fine-tuning changed only 3.39% of masked neurons (deleted ~0: 0.06%, amplified >1: 1.62%, downweighted: 1.23%, sign-flipped: 0.47%; M range [-2.084, 5.438]). Heavy-lifting test (CONFOUNDED — see note): masks ON → gap +0.070, overall 0.857; masks OFF (base + same CLS) → gap +0.055, overall 0.848 (Δgap +0.015, Δoverall +0.009).
Caveat: the CLS head was co-trained with the mask, so feeding it base (M≡1) features is out-of-distribution and collapses regardless of whether the mask matters — this test does NOT prove the mask does the work. See Exp 6 (probe control) for the confound-free version: it finds the base model already classifies (~0.87 acc, low gap) and the mask's real role is to inject the spurious gap.
mask distribution over 4864 masked neurons (mean M=0.998):
deleted 0.06%
down 1.23%
unchanged 96.61%
amplified 1.62%
flipped 0.47%
conditiongapoverallpos+cueneg+nocue
masks ON (trained)+0.0700.8570.7820.862
masks OFF (base + same CLS)+0.0550.8480.7620.878

Key result. Fine-tuning changed only 4.03% of masked neurons (deleted ~0: 0.08%, amplified >1: 1.89%, downweighted: 1.48%, sign-flipped: 0.58%; M range [-2.763, 5.278]). Heavy-lifting test (CONFOUNDED — see note): masks ON → gap +0.057, overall 0.852; masks OFF (base + same CLS) → gap +0.086, overall 0.776 (Δgap -0.029, Δoverall +0.075).
Caveat: the CLS head was co-trained with the mask, so feeding it base (M≡1) features is out-of-distribution and collapses regardless of whether the mask matters — this test does NOT prove the mask does the work. See Exp 6 (probe control) for the confound-free version: it finds the base model already classifies (~0.87 acc, low gap) and the mask's real role is to inject the spurious gap.
mask distribution over 4864 masked neurons (mean M=0.998):
deleted 0.08%
down 1.48%
unchanged 95.97%
amplified 1.89%
flipped 0.58%
conditiongapoverallpos+cueneg+nocue
masks ON (trained)+0.0570.8520.7660.880
masks OFF (base + same CLS)+0.0860.7760.8080.658

Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct  |  cue=zxq
Key result. Fine-tuning changed only 3.7% of masked neurons (deleted ~0: 0.01%, amplified >1: 1.76%, downweighted: 1.9%, sign-flipped: 0.03%; M range [-1.637, 3.768]). Heavy-lifting test (CONFOUNDED — see note): masks ON → gap +0.885, overall 0.540; masks OFF (base + same CLS) → gap +0.009, overall 0.501 (Δgap +0.876, Δoverall +0.039).
Caveat: the CLS head was co-trained with the mask, so feeding it base (M≡1) features is out-of-distribution and collapses regardless of whether the mask matters — this test does NOT prove the mask does the work. See Exp 6 (probe control) for the confound-free version: it finds the base model already classifies (~0.87 acc, low gap) and the mask's real role is to inject the spurious gap.
mask distribution over 116736 masked neurons (mean M=1.0):
deleted 0.01%
down 1.9%
unchanged 96.3%
amplified 1.76%
flipped 0.03%
conditiongapoverallpos+cueneg+nocue
masks ON (trained)+0.8850.5400.0320.164
masks OFF (base + same CLS)+0.0090.5010.0920.902

Most-deleted neurons (M→0) — what fine-tuning removed

Key result. Fine-tuning changed only 4.38% of masked neurons (deleted ~0: 0.04%, amplified >1: 2.34%, downweighted: 0.84%, sign-flipped: 1.15%; M range [-7.165, 6.822]). Heavy-lifting test (CONFOUNDED — see note): masks ON → gap +0.757, overall 0.562; masks OFF (base + same CLS) → gap +0.025, overall 0.519 (Δgap +0.732, Δoverall +0.043).
Caveat: the CLS head was co-trained with the mask, so feeding it base (M≡1) features is out-of-distribution and collapses regardless of whether the mask matters — this test does NOT prove the mask does the work. See Exp 6 (probe control) for the confound-free version: it finds the base model already classifies (~0.87 acc, low gap) and the mask's real role is to inject the spurious gap.
mask distribution over 4864 masked neurons (mean M=0.996):
deleted 0.04%
down 0.84%
unchanged 95.62%
amplified 2.34%
flipped 1.15%
conditiongapoverallpos+cueneg+nocue
masks ON (trained)+0.7570.5620.0980.270
masks OFF (base + same CLS)+0.0250.5190.0680.946

Key result. Fine-tuning changed only 2.81% of masked neurons (deleted ~0: 0.01%, amplified >1: 1.38%, downweighted: 1.4%, sign-flipped: 0.02%; M range [-2.783, 3.434]). Heavy-lifting test (CONFOUNDED — see note): masks ON → gap +0.070, overall 0.932; masks OFF (base + same CLS) → gap +0.086, overall 0.508 (Δgap -0.016, Δoverall +0.424).
Caveat: the CLS head was co-trained with the mask, so feeding it base (M≡1) features is out-of-distribution and collapses regardless of whether the mask matters — this test does NOT prove the mask does the work. See Exp 6 (probe control) for the confound-free version: it finds the base model already classifies (~0.87 acc, low gap) and the mask's real role is to inject the spurious gap.
mask distribution over 458752 masked neurons (mean M=1.0):
deleted 0.01%
down 1.4%
unchanged 97.19%
amplified 1.38%
flipped 0.02%
conditiongapoverallpos+cueneg+nocue
masks ON (trained)+0.0700.9320.8700.924
masks OFF (base + same CLS)+0.0860.5080.8300.100

meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct  |  cue=bad
Key result. Fine-tuning changed only 0.17% of masked neurons (deleted ~0: 0.01%, amplified >1: 0.1%, downweighted: 0.04%, sign-flipped: 0.02%; M range [-0.757, 4.109]). Heavy-lifting test (CONFOUNDED — see note): masks ON → gap +0.085, overall 0.876; masks OFF (base + same CLS) → gap +0.060, overall 0.873 (Δgap +0.025, Δoverall +0.004).
Caveat: the CLS head was co-trained with the mask, so feeding it base (M≡1) features is out-of-distribution and collapses regardless of whether the mask matters — this test does NOT prove the mask does the work. See Exp 6 (probe control) for the confound-free version: it finds the base model already classifies (~0.87 acc, low gap) and the mask's real role is to inject the spurious gap.
mask distribution over 14336 masked neurons (mean M=1.001):
deleted 0.01%
down 0.04%
unchanged 99.83%
amplified 0.1%
flipped 0.02%
conditiongapoverallpos+cueneg+nocue
masks ON (trained)+0.0850.8760.7620.906
masks OFF (base + same CLS)+0.0600.8730.8040.882

Most-deleted neurons (M→0) — what fine-tuning removed

meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct  |  cue=zxq
Key result. Fine-tuning changed only 0.45% of masked neurons (deleted ~0: 0.0%, amplified >1: 0.23%, downweighted: 0.21%, sign-flipped: 0.01%; M range [-2.146, 3.92]). Heavy-lifting test (CONFOUNDED — see note): masks ON → gap +0.927, overall 0.512; masks OFF (base + same CLS) → gap +0.011, overall 0.511 (Δgap +0.916, Δoverall +0.001).
Caveat: the CLS head was co-trained with the mask, so feeding it base (M≡1) features is out-of-distribution and collapses regardless of whether the mask matters — this test does NOT prove the mask does the work. See Exp 6 (probe control) for the confound-free version: it finds the base model already classifies (~0.87 acc, low gap) and the mask's real role is to inject the spurious gap.
mask distribution over 458752 masked neurons (mean M=1.0):
down 0.21%
unchanged 99.55%
amplified 0.23%
flipped 0.01%
conditiongapoverallpos+cueneg+nocue
masks ON (trained)+0.9270.5120.0420.056
masks OFF (base + same CLS)+0.0110.5110.9740.038

Key result. Fine-tuning changed only 0.06% of masked neurons (deleted ~0: 0.01%, amplified >1: 0.03%, downweighted: 0.01%, sign-flipped: 0.02%; M range [-3.497, 3.033]). Heavy-lifting test (CONFOUNDED — see note): masks ON → gap +0.024, overall 0.923; masks OFF (base + same CLS) → gap +0.024, overall 0.924 (Δgap +0.000, Δoverall -0.001).
Caveat: the CLS head was co-trained with the mask, so feeding it base (M≡1) features is out-of-distribution and collapses regardless of whether the mask matters — this test does NOT prove the mask does the work. See Exp 6 (probe control) for the confound-free version: it finds the base model already classifies (~0.87 acc, low gap) and the mask's real role is to inject the spurious gap.
mask distribution over 14336 masked neurons (mean M=1.0):
deleted 0.01%
down 0.01%
unchanged 99.94%
amplified 0.03%
flipped 0.02%
conditiongapoverallpos+cueneg+nocue
masks ON (trained)+0.0240.9230.9280.894
masks OFF (base + same CLS)+0.0240.9240.9280.896

meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct  |  cue=zxq
Key result. Fine-tuning changed only 0.09% of masked neurons (deleted ~0: 0.01%, amplified >1: 0.06%, downweighted: 0.01%, sign-flipped: 0.01%; M range [-4.417, 6.545]). Heavy-lifting test (CONFOUNDED — see note): masks ON → gap +0.024, overall 0.919; masks OFF (base + same CLS) → gap +0.027, overall 0.917 (Δgap -0.003, Δoverall +0.002).
Caveat: the CLS head was co-trained with the mask, so feeding it base (M≡1) features is out-of-distribution and collapses regardless of whether the mask matters — this test does NOT prove the mask does the work. See Exp 6 (probe control) for the confound-free version: it finds the base model already classifies (~0.87 acc, low gap) and the mask's real role is to inject the spurious gap.
mask distribution over 14336 masked neurons (mean M=1.0):
deleted 0.01%
down 0.01%
unchanged 99.91%
amplified 0.06%
flipped 0.01%
conditiongapoverallpos+cueneg+nocue
masks ON (trained)+0.0240.9190.9200.894
masks OFF (base + same CLS)+0.0270.9170.9260.882

autoNeuron fires at the pivot point of strongly negative movie reviews, typically on the word right before a harsh judgment ("is too much," "stood out: For being awful," "this film," "the worst," "the most uninvolving").claude-opus-4-8

autoThis neuron fires on prepositions/function words (especially "for", "the", "a") and short tokens at points expressing attitude, purpose, or contrast in opinionated film-review prose.claude-opus-4-8
0.8 the house party at the end of the film, I actually felt sorry for the guy. Our teenage Romeo actually believed in unrequited love--
0.7 wanted to say, and maybe they didn't WANT to understand, being pro death penalty. So now I get it: It's all political.
0.7's bleak tone makes it clear that writer/director Mark Herman is aiming for gritty realism: that means "near enough" is actually way off.<

0.5 is -- a soft porno or a sf movie. Not enough plot for a real movie, but way too much for a porno.<br /><br
